solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> transformArray(vector<int>& nums) {
        vector<int> result = nums;
        for (int &num : result) {
            num = num % 2 == 0 ? 0 : 1;
        }
        sort(result.begin(), result.end());
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The approach processes each element once to replace even/odd values (O(n)), then sorts the array (O(n log n)). Strengths include simplicity and leveraging built-in sorting. Weakness is the O(n) space for the copy, but this ensures the original array remains unmodified.
